pdg_node.informal_desc: Creates a copy of the input signal, truncates any values below zero to rectify it, and computes the square of the result. This enhances features for peak detection.

declaration.name: preprocess_signal
declaration.docstring: Creates a copy of the input signal, truncates any values below zero to rectify it, and computes the square of the result. This enhances features for peak detection.

pdg_node.statement: (squared_signal: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float, beatwindow: float) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Calculates two moving average filters over the squared signal using different window sizes (peakwindow and beatwindow) to create distinct signal envelopes for thresholding and block identification.

declaration.name: compute_signal_envelopes
declaration.type_signature: (squared_signal: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float, beatwindow: float) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
declaration.docstring: Calculates two moving average filters over the squared signal using different window sizes (peakwindow and beatwindow) to create distinct signal envelopes for thresholding and block identification.

declaration.docstring: Calculates two moving average filters over the squared signal using different window sizes (peakwindow and beatwindow) to create distinct signal envelopes for thresholding and block identification.

pdg_node.statement: (beat_envelope: np.ndarray, squared_signal: np.ndarray, beatoffset: float) -> np.ndarray
pdg_node.informal_desc: Computes a dynamic threshold by combining the beat envelope with a scaled mean of the squared signal's energy. This allows the threshold to adapt to local signal variations.

declaration.name: calculate_dynamic_threshold
declaration.type_signature: (beat_envelope: np.ndarray, squared_signal: np.ndarray, beatoffset: float) -> np.ndarray
declaration.docstring: Computes a dynamic threshold by combining the beat envelope with a scaled mean of the squared signal's energy. This allows the threshold to adapt to local signal variations.

declaration.name: calculate_dynamic_threshold
declaration.type_signature: (beat_envelope: np.ndarray, squared_signal: np.ndarray, beatoffset: float) -> np.ndarray
declaration.docstring: Computes a dynamic threshold by combining the beat envelope with a scaled mean of the squared signal's energy. This allows the threshold to adapt to local signal variations.

declaration.name: calculate_dynamic_threshold
declaration.type_signature: (beat_envelope: np.ndarray, squared_signal: np.ndarray, beatoffset: float) -> np.ndarray
declaration.docstring: Computes a dynamic threshold by combining the beat envelope with a scaled mean of the squared signal's energy. This allows the threshold to adapt to local signal variations.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Identifies continuous blocks of interest ('waves') where the peak envelope exceeds the dynamic threshold. These blocks are candidates for containing signal onsets.

declaration.name: identify_candidate_blocks
declaration.docstring: Identifies continuous blocks of interest ('waves') where the peak envelope exceeds the dynamic threshold. These blocks are candidates for containing signal onsets.

pdg_node.statement: (processed_signal: np.ndarray, candidate_blocks: list[tuple[int, int]], sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float) -> list[int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Filters candidate blocks by a minimum length criterion and then, within each valid block, finds the single most prominent peak in the processed signal.

declaration.name: extract_prominent_peaks_from_blocks
declaration.type_signature: (processed_signal: np.ndarray, candidate_blocks: list[tuple[int, int]], sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float) -> list[int]
declaration.docstring: Filters candidate blocks by a minimum length criterion and then, within each valid block, finds the single most prominent peak in the processed signal.

pdg_node.statement: (prominent_peaks: list[int], sampling_rate: float, mindelay: float) -> np.ndarray
pdg_node.informal_desc: Filters the list of prominent peaks to ensure that the time delay between any two consecutive peaks is above a minimum threshold. This removes falsely detected adjacent peaks.

declaration.name: filter_peaks_by_minimum_delay
declaration.type_signature: (prominent_peaks: list[int], sampling_rate: float, mindelay: float) -> np.ndarray
declaration.docstring: Filters the list of prominent peaks to ensure that the time delay between any two consecutive peaks is above a minimum threshold. This removes falsely detected adjacent peaks.

declaration.type_signature: (prominent_peaks: list[int], sampling_rate: float, mindelay: float) -> np.ndarray
